_doc: Ordered DHCP reservation plan for the F6600P (issue #170, phase 1 = planner only, no router writes). Copy this file to config/dhcp_plan.json (gitignored — it would expose your device inventory in a public repo) and edit the ranges/rules to taste. The planner (src/dhcp_plan.py) reads it to assign each known LAN device a stable IP inside its category range; you apply the result manually in the router's DHCP Binding form. When config/dhcp_plan.json is absent the planner returns an empty plan (every device unassigned) with a warning rather than failing.
_doc_ranges: Ordered category ranges. Only the last octet is planned; each range is inclusive [start, end]. Keep them within the router's DHCP pool (192.168.0.10–192.168.0.250). A device gets the lowest free IP in its category's range, so an IP tells you what a device is.
_doc_rules: How a device auto-classifies into a category. Each rule's `match` is a list of lowercase substrings tested against the device's "display-name hostname vendor" text; the FIRST rule with any hit wins. `category` must equal one of the range labels above. Reorder rules so the more specific ones come first.
_doc_overrides: Manual per-MAC escape hatch (highest priority — beats the rules). MAC keys are upper-case, colon-separated. Value must equal one of the range labels above. Use this for devices the keyword rules can't classify (e.g. a generic n/a hostname).
